Late Round Prospect Guide — Evaluation 2022–2025

Nick Gurol

Executive summary

We define the ground-truth tier from realized production (best PPR FFPG, Y1–Y3 post-draft):

Production tier Cutoff (PPR PPG) Verbal anchor
Elite ≥ 16.5 RB1 / WR1 cornerstone
Starter 12 – 16.5 RB2 / WR2 (weekly start)
Flex 7 – 12 FLEX-worthy
Depth 3 – 7 bench
Dart Throw < 3 irrelevant

We compare JJ’s predicted tier (canonical_tier from his guide) to this production tier, alongside a baseline tier derived from lm(best_ffppg ~ age + log(draft_pick)) binned with the same cutoffs.

Headline: JJ’s canonical_tier vs baseline lm(best_ffppg ~ age + log(draft_pick)) binned via same cutoffs.
Position N JJ accuracy Baseline accuracy JJ κ Baseline κ JJ weighted F1 Baseline weighted F1 JJ off-by-one Baseline off-by-one
WR 127 0.370 0.370 0.487 0.508 0.347 0.362 0.811 0.890
RB 96 0.448 0.385 0.475 0.562 0.435 0.391 0.802 0.875

Headline: On both positions, the age + log(draft_pick) baseline matches or beats JJ’s canonical tier on quadratic kappa and off-by-one rate — confirming the regression result that draft capital captures most of his signal. The interesting story is in the per-tier breakdown below: where exactly he wins and loses.

Methodology evolution 2022–2025

The guide evolved markedly across the four years studied.

  • 2022–2024 (Z-Prospect Model): Numeric tiers 1–15 (2022, 2023) or 1–12 (2024) drawn directly from the post-draft rookie rankings cheatsheet. No verbal tier semantics beyond ordinal position.
  • 2025–2026 (ZAP Model): Verbal tiers (Elite Producer → Dart Throw, plus a 2026-only Legendary Performer top tier). Each tier carries a production claim (e.g., “Weekly Starter — should provide stability in your starting lineup, think RB2/WR2”).

Tier definitions — his and ours

How JJ defines each tier

The verbal tier scheme (2025–2026) is the only year-range where JJ publishes explicit definitions. Each tier is a band of the ZAP Score (his model output, 0–100 scale; higher = better prospect):

Canonical bucket JJ’s verbal tier (2025–2026) Stated ZAP score band JJ’s production claim
Elite Legendary Performer (2026 only) 90–100 “Generational talent. Hit at outstanding rates historically. Cornerstone dynasty asset.”
Elite Elite Producer 75–90 “Best of the best. Legitimate shot at a cornerstone piece.”
Starter Weekly Starter 60–75 “Stability in your starting lineup. Think RB2/WR2.”
Flex Flex Play 40–60 “Rarely become league-winners. Can provide usable production.”
Depth Benchwarmer 30–40 “Perfect for bench insurance.”
Depth Waiver Wire Add 20–30 “Probably doesn’t need to be rostered, but can occasionally be usable.”
Dart Throw Dart Throw 0–20 “Very little chance for success.”

For 2022–2024, JJ uses purely numeric tiers from his Post-Draft Rookie Rankings Cheatsheet (Tier 1 → Tier 15 in 2022/2023; Tier 1 → Tier 12 in 2024 + an “Unranked” bucket for players he profiled but didn’t rank). There is no verbal label or stated FFPG/ZAP cutoff per tier in those years — the “definition” is just rank order in the cheatsheet.

How WE map his tiers to our canonical 5-tier scheme

To compare across years, we collapse every year’s tier nomenclature to {Elite, Starter, Flex, Depth, Dart Throw} (canonical 5-tier scheme). The full mapping lives in extraction/output/tier_map.json; here’s the summary by year:

Canonical 2022 (numeric 1–15) 2023 (Tier 1–15) 2024 (Tier 1–12 + Unranked) 2025 / 2026 (verbal)
Elite 1 Tier 1 Tier 1 Legendary Performer / Elite Producer
Starter 2–3 Tier 2–3 Tier 2–3 Weekly Starter
Flex 4–6 Tier 4–6 Tier 4–5 Flex Play
Depth 7–9 Tier 7–9 Tier 6–8 Benchwarmer / Waiver Wire Add
Dart Throw 10–15 + Z-Prospect Ranked Tier 10–15 Tier 9–12 + Unranked Dart Throw

Numeric tiers (2022–2024) were collapsed by proportional bucketing: ~top 7% → Elite, next ~15% → Starter, etc. The harmonizer’s full rationale is in tier_map.json under the rationale key.

Empirical ZAP score distribution by canonical tier (2024–2026)

What ZAP score does each canonical tier actually carry, year by year? (Players without ZAP scores — 2022/2023 cheatsheet-only — are excluded.)

Mean ZAP score by canonical tier and year (WR+RB only; 2022/2023 NA)
guide_year Elite Starter Flex Depth Dart Throw
2024 99.3 97.8 94.6 81.7 45.2
2025 97.0 88.7 75.6 50.5 13.3
2026 87.0 65.4 47.3 27.5 8.8

The 2024 mapping looks compressed near the top because 2024 used many fine-grained numeric tiers; our canonical 5-tier collapse puts everyone Tier 1–5 into Elite/Starter/Flex, all with ZAP scores above 80. 2025/2026 verbal tiers spread across the full ZAP range more evenly.

Coverage 2022–2026

WR + RB players per guide year
guide_year RB WR
2022 25 26
2023 15 22
2024 29 39
2025 31 48
2026 21 46

Note on 2026: the 2026 class was extracted and harmonized but is excluded from all eval metrics. Player birthdates aren’t yet in nflreadr for these rookies, and even if matched, they have no NFL season data yet.

Production tier distribution

How many of each FFPG-defined tier did 2022–2025 rookies actually produce?

Production tier counts (eval set, 2022-2025)
position Dart Throw Depth Flex Starter Elite
RB 44 21 15 12 7
WR 48 32 30 15 7

The boxplot still uses JJ’s canonical_tier on the x-axis — it shows that his Elite/Starter calls do produce higher PPG on average, just with massive variance.

How JJ’s tier compares to the production tier

Below we compare JJ’s predicted canonical_tier (from his guide) directly to the FFPG-defined production_tier (truth). For reference we also compare a baseline that predicts FFPG from age + log(draft_pick) and bins the prediction with the same cutoffs.

Confusion matrices

Each row is the true tier (production-derived); each column is the predicted tier. Diagonal = correct, off-diagonal = miss.

Per-tier precision, recall, F1

WR

JJ’s predictions:

tier support precision recall f1
Dart Throw 43 0.534 0.721 0.614
Depth 32 0.207 0.188 0.197
Flex 30 0.261 0.200 0.226
Starter 15 0.273 0.200 0.231
Elite 7 0.167 0.143 0.154

Baseline (age + log capital) predictions:

tier support precision recall f1
Dart Throw 43 0.706 0.279 0.400
Depth 32 0.286 0.562 0.379
Flex 30 0.342 0.433 0.382
Starter 15 0.500 0.267 0.348
Elite 7 0.000 0.000 NA

RB

JJ’s predictions:

tier support precision recall f1
Dart Throw 41 0.574 0.659 0.614
Depth 21 0.346 0.429 0.383
Flex 15 0.167 0.133 0.148
Starter 12 0.429 0.250 0.316
Elite 7 0.500 0.286 0.364

Baseline (age + log capital) predictions:

tier support precision recall f1
Dart Throw 41 0.812 0.317 0.456
Depth 21 0.280 0.667 0.394
Flex 15 0.261 0.400 0.316
Starter 12 0.500 0.167 0.250
Elite 7 0.667 0.286 0.400

Read: Precision answers “of players he tagged tier X, what fraction actually became tier X.” Recall answers “of players who actually were tier X, what fraction did he catch.” Both predictors over-call Dart Throw and Depth because most rookies bust — the meaningful question is the Starter/Elite rows.

Tier-call calibration: do his Elite calls actually become Elite?

The diagonal cells of the confusion matrix above answer this directly. Pulling out the high-stakes tiers (Starter + Elite, where his model is essentially “this guy is fantasy-relevant”):

Calibration: for each predicted tier, what fraction of calls were exactly correct vs. at-or-above the predicted tier.
position predicted_tier n_called frac_at_or_above frac_exact predictor
RB Elite 3 67% 67% Baseline
RB Starter 4 75% 50% Baseline
RB Flex 23 61% 26% Baseline
RB Depth 50 54% 28% Baseline
RB Dart Throw 16 100% 81% Baseline

RB Starter 7 57% 43% JJ
RB Flex 12 58% 17% JJ
RB Depth 26 62% 35% JJ
RB Dart Throw 47 100% 57% JJ
WR Elite 1 0% 0% Baseline
WR Starter 8 75% 50% Baseline
WR Flex 38 63% 34% Baseline
WR Depth 63 54% 29% Baseline
WR Dart Throw 17 100% 71% Baseline
WR Elite 6 17% 17% JJ
WR Starter 11 45% 27% JJ
WR Flex 23 57% 26% JJ
WR Depth 29 62% 21% JJ
WR Dart Throw 58 100% 53% JJ

Regression — supplementary

The original regression-style read on the question “does his tier add over draft capital + age?” Same conclusion as the classification: no statistically significant signal.

Position N Baseline adj R² + canonical_tier adj R² ΔR² F-test p
WR 127 0.40 0.39 -0.00 0.514
RB 96 0.39 0.38 -0.02 0.859

Sleeper detection — when JJ disagrees with draft capital

Operational definition: a JJ sleeper is a player where JJ’s ZAP score (0–100) is higher than the draft-capital-and-age baseline score (also 0–100, percentile-ranked within position). The baseline is lm(best_ffppg ~ age + log(draft_pick)) predictions, ranked.

JJ score is on a 0-100 scale: ZAP score directly for 2024-2026 (where JJ publishes it per-player), and a tier midpoint for 2022-2023 (where the cheatsheet only gives ordinal tier; we use JJ’s own stated 2026 ZAP-band midpoints — Dart Throw=10, Depth=30, Flex=50, Starter=67.5, Elite=87.5). Baseline score is the percentile rank within position of lm(best_ffppg ~ age + log(draft_pick)) predictions.

Coverage: 223 total players in the score table — 137 with native ZAP (2024-25), 86 with tier-midpoint fallback (2022-23).

How often does JJ disagree with draft capital?

Counts of (JJ score) vs (baseline percentile score) disagreement, 2022-2025 full eval set
position JJ more bearish (-10 or worse) JJ more bullish (+10 or more) Roughly aligned (within +-10)
RB 39 26 31
WR 45 43 39

The scatter — score-vs-score, colored by actual production tier

Each point is a 2022–2025 rookie. The diagonal is “JJ agrees with draft capital.” Points above = JJ-bumps; below = JJ-fades. Color = actual production tier. Triangles = day-3/UDFA, circles = early-round. Top 5 biggest disagreements each way (per position) are name-labeled.

Outcomes by JJ-bump magnitude

If JJ has any independent signal, players he scores meaningfully higher than the baseline should hit Starter+ at higher rates than players where he agrees with the baseline (or fades).

Outcomes by JJ-vs-baseline gap bucket — full eval set (2024-2025)
position bump_bucket n mean_ffppg starter_plus_rate elite_rate bust_rate

WR Neutral (within +-10) 39 5.62 13% 5% 44%

Ranking quality — for dynasty drafting

If you’re using one of these rankings to draft (1.01, 1.02, 1.03…), how well does each one order players against actual production? Three views.

Spearman rank correlation with actual best_ffppg

Spearman rank correlation of each score with actual best_ffppg.
Position N Spearman: JJ vs actual Spearman: Baseline vs actual
WR 127 0.337 0.669
RB 96 0.269 0.616

Top-K precision — of the top K by each ranking, what fraction were truly top K?

Of the top K players each ranker selected, fraction that were actually top K by best_ffppg.
position k jj_precision baseline_precision
WR 5 20% 20%
WR 10 20% 50%
WR 20 30% 60%
WR 30 33% 63%
RB 5 0% 40%
RB 10 20% 40%
RB 20 35% 60%
RB 30 40% 63%

Cumulative production curve

If you drafted strictly in each ranker’s order, this is the total best_ffppg you would have accumulated as you went down the list. Steeper = better ranker (picks the productive players first). The dashed line is the oracle upper bound (ranking by actual production).

Pearson correlation with continuous best_ffppg

Pearson correlation of each score with continuous best_ffppg. pearson_zap_only_2024_25 restricts to ZAP-available years for a like-for-like ZAP-vs-baseline comparison.
position n_total n_with_zap pearson_jj pearson_baseline pearson_zap_only_2024_25
WR 127 79 0.301 0.630 0.616
RB 96 58 0.264 0.586 0.474
